question: Does the image show exactly three domed building cross-section models displayed side by side?
Is the arrangement of the buildings strictly from left to right: Roman Pantheon, Hagia Sophia, Florence Cathedral?
Are the three models displayed at a roughly uniform scale relative to one another?
Are all three buildings presented as cross-section models, revealing internal wall thickness and structure?
Is the leftmost model recognizable as the Roman Pantheon, featuring a hemispherical dome with a central oculus (circular opening)?
Does the Pantheon model depict coffered (recessed panel) patterns on the interior dome surface?
Is the center model recognizable as the Hagia Sophia, featuring a large central dome supported by semi-domes or a cascading dome system?
Is the rightmost model recognizable as Brunelleschi's Dome (Florence Cathedral), featuring a distinct octagonal shape and pointed profile?
Does the Florence Cathedral model show visible structural ribs or a double-shell construction?
Are the models placed on simple pedestals within a realistic museum exhibition hall interior?
Does the image exhibit soft, diffused museum-style lighting?
Is the image free of any text labels, placards, or signage?
Is the image free of logos and watermarks?
Are the structural details of the domes free from obvious geometric distortions or nonsensical artifacts?
